title: Integrity protection of control plane operation
abstract: Various aspects of the present disclosure relate to network entity storing code comprising instructions executable to cause the network entity to: receive a request message, the request message indicating a requirement to protect the integrity of a control plane operation in a wireless communication network using a distributed ledger technology (DLT); obtain a policy based on the request message, the policy defining a predefined action to be performed upon detection of a trigger event associated with the wireless communication network, the predefined action comprising the control plane operation; identify a plurality of nodes to participate in the control plane operation based on the request message, the plurality of nodes coupled to a DLT network; and transmit the policy to the plurality of nodes.
claims:
Claims 1. A network entity storing code comprising instructions executable to cause the network entity to: receive a request message, the request message indicating a requirement to protect the integrity of a control plane operation in a wireless communication network using a distributed ledger technology (DLT); obtain a policy based on the request message, the policy defining a predefined action to be performed upon detection of a trigger event associated with the wireless communication network, the predefined action comprising the control plane operation; identify a plurality of nodes to participate in the control plane operation based on the request message, the plurality of nodes coupled to a DLT network; and transmit the policy to the plurality of nodes. 2. The network entity of claim 1, wherein the policy is prestored in memory accessible to the network entity, and the instructions are further executable to cause the network entity to obtain the policy from the memory. 3. The network entity of claim 1 , wherein the instructions are further executable to cause the network entity to generate the policy based on the request message. 4. The network entity of any preceding claim, wherein the request message comprises an identifier of each of the plurality of nodes. 5. The network entity of any preceding claim, wherein the request message comprises an identifier of the control plane operation. 6. The network entity of any preceding claim, wherein the request message comprises one or more of: (i) a time and/or area of validity of the requirement, (ii) exposure requirements for interaction with nodes external to the DLT network, (iii) an indication of a radio access technology to be used for the control plane operation, (iv) an indication of a 

public land mobile network to be used for the control plane operation, (v) spectrum considerations to be used for the control plane operation, and (vi) one or more performance requirements for the control plane operation. 7. The network entity of any preceding claim, wherein the policy is a smart contract. 8. The network entity of any preceding claim, wherein the instructions are further executable to cause the network entity to transmit at least one configuration message to a gateway node to enable one or more of the plurality of nodes to communicate with nodes external to the DLT network. 9. The network entity of claim 8, wherein the at least one configuration message comprises: (i) the policy and (ii) an identifier of each of the plurality of nodes. 10. The network entity of claim 9, wherein the at least one configuration message further comprises exposure requirements for interaction with the nodes external to the DLT network. 11. The network entity of any preceding claim, wherein the network entity is a near realtime radio access network (RAN) intelligent controller, or is a network entity configured to communicate with a near real-time RAN intelligent controller. 12. The network entity of any of claims 1 to 10, wherein the network entity is a non -realtime radio access network (RAN) intelligent controller. 13. The network entity of any of claims 1 to 10, wherein the network entity is a Multiaccess Edge Computing (MEC) host. 14. A processor for wireless communication, comprising: at least one controller coupled with at least one memory and configured to cause the processor to: 

 obtain a request message, the request message indicating a requirement to protect the integrity of a control plane operation in a wireless communication network using a distributed ledger technology (DLT); obtain a policy based on the request message, the policy defining a predefined action to be performed upon detection of a trigger event associated with the wireless communication network, the predefined action comprising the control plane operation; identify a plurality of nodes to participate in the control plane operation based on the request message, the plurality of nodes coupled to a DLT network; and output the policy for transmission to the plurality of nodes. 15. A method performed by a network entity, the method comprising: receiving a request message, the request message indicating a requirement to protect the integrity of a control plane operation in a wireless communication network using a distributed ledger technology (DLT); obtaining a policy based on the request message, the policy defining a predefined action to be performed upon detection of a trigger event associated with the wireless communication network, the predefined action comprising the control plane operation; identifying a plurality of nodes to participate in the control plane operation based on the request message, the plurality of nodes coupled to a DLT network; and transmitting the policy to the plurality of nodes. 16. A network entity storing code comprising instructions executable to cause the network entity to: receive at least one configuration message which comprises: (i) a policy, the policy defining a predefined action to be performed by a node coupled to a distributed ledger technology (DLT) network upon detection of a trigger event in a wireless communication network, the predefined action comprising a control plane operation in the wireless communication network, and (ii) an identifier of each of a plurality of nodes to participate in the control plane operation in the wireless communication network, the plurality of nodes coupled to the DLT network; and 

 register an application programming interface (API) at an API registry, the API enabling the network entity to receive data from one or more nodes external to the DLT network for transmission to the node coupled to the DLT network for detection of the trigger event. 17. The network entity of claim 16, wherein the network entity comprises the API registry, wherein the instructions are further executable to cause the network entity to transmit API information associated with the API to the one or more nodes external to the DLT network participating in the control plane operation. 18. The network entity of claim 16 or 17, wherein the at least one configuration message further comprises interaction information, the interaction information defining (i) information to be transmitted by the network entity to one or more nodes external to the DLT network in response to detection of the control plane operation, and (ii) an identifier of each of the one or more nodes external to the DLT network. 19. The network entity of any of claims 16 to 18, wherein the at least one configuration message further comprises an identifier of the control plane operation.
